title: Gua sha facial oil blend
subtitle: A lightweight anhydrous facial oil blended for gua sha massage, with squalane, rosehip, and frankincense
excerpt: Gua sha practice requires a facial oil that provides enough slip for the stone to glide without dragging, but is light enough to absorb rather than sit on top of the skin. Squalane is the ideal base: non-comedogenic, almost weightless, and stable. Rosehip and a small amount of frankincense essential oil complete the blend.
sourceNotes: Gua sha facial oil blend. Squalane (from olive or sugarcane): very stable, low comedogenic rating, ideal slip agent for stone tools. Rosehip oil (Rosa canina): high linoleic acid content; shelf life shorter than squalane so vitamin E added at 0.5%. Frankincense (Boswellia carterii) essential oil at 0.5% for a subtle resinous note; below IFRA limit for face products. Total blend 30 ml fills a standard 30 ml dropper bottle.

recipe.yieldDescription: ~30 ml facial oil (fills a 30 ml amber dropper bottle)
recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. Keep frankincense essential oil at 0.5% or below for facial use.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 6 months in an amber glass bottle away from light. The squalane is very stable; rosehip oil limits the shelf life. Discard if the oil smells rancid.

glossaryTerms.definition: A traditional East Asian massage technique using a smooth-edged tool (often jade or rose quartz) pressed and stroked against oiled skin. Used on the face to promote circulation and ease muscle tension in the jaw and brow.

A lightweight, stable oil derived from olive or sugarcane. Non-comedogenic, odourless, and absorbs quickly without greasiness. The ideal slip agent for facial massage tools as it glides smoothly and does not clog pores.

content.text: requires a facial oil that lets the stone tool glide smoothly without dragging the skin.

is the best base for this: almost weightless, very stable, and non-comedogenic. Rosehip oil adds fatty acids and a subtle colour. Frankincense essential oil gives a light resinous note. Vitamin E extends the rosehip oil's shelf life. No water phase means no preservative is needed.

items.prepNote: 60%; from olive or sugarcane; main slip agent

content.text: Weigh all ingredients into the beaker. Stir to combine. Transfer to an amber dropper bottle and cap. Turn gently a few times to mix fully.
Apply 3 to 5 drops to clean skin and spread evenly over the face and neck. Use the gua sha tool with light, upward strokes. Follow with your usual moisturiser if needed.
6 months in an amber bottle away from light. Discard if the oil smells rancid.
